skill.name: web-navigation
skill.description: Navigation and routing patterns for React web applications. Use when implementing React Router, Next.js routing, deep links, or handling navigation state.
skill.summary: Navigation and routing patterns for React web applications. Use when implementing React Router, Next...
skill.author: CJHarmath

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer reported many command execution and high-risk patterns, but review found they are false positives from Markdown code fences, ordinary routing text, and TypeScript string literals. No prompt injection, malicious intent, data exfiltration, filesystem access, or actual shell execution was found. One low-risk example uses an unsanitized redirect query parameter that could encourage unsafe app code if copied without validation.

low_findings.title: Unvalidated Redirect Parameter Example
low_findings.description: A login example reads a redirect value from search parameters and navigates to it without showing validation. This is not malicious skill behavior, but users should restrict redirects to trusted internal paths when adapting the snippet.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code directly uses searchParams.get('redirect') as a navigation destination. The finding is low risk because it is documentation content, not executable marketplace code.

low_findings.title: Static Findings Dismissed as Markdown and Text
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby backtick execution findings are Markdown code fences, not shell execution. The weak cryptographic algorithm and reconnaissance findings are ordinary words, route examples, or TypeScript literals with no cryptographic or system probing behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are documentation examples and fenced Markdown blocks. No executable Ruby, shell command, cryptographic API, system command, or prompt injection text was found.

dangerous_patterns.title: Copy-Paste Open Redirect Pattern
dangerous_patterns.description: The redirect-after-login example should validate the redirect target before navigation. Without validation, a real application could allow navigation to an unexpected destination.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The unsafe pattern is clearly present in an example snippet. Impact depends on how a user adapts the documentation in a real application.

content.value_statement: Modern React apps need predictable routing, deep links, and navigation state. This skill provides practical React Router and Next.js patterns for common web navigation tasks.
actual_capabilities: Shows React Router v6 setup with nested, dynamic, and protected routes.
Explains Next.js App Router layouts, dynamic routes, and route groups.
Provides examples for programmatic navigation and link components.
Demonstrates query parameter handling for filters, tabs, and deep links.
Covers loading states, error states, scroll restoration, and navigation guards.
limitations: It is a documentation skill, not an executable routing library.
Examples may need updates for project-specific framework versions.
Authentication and authorization logic are shown only at a pattern level.
Security validation for redirects and user-controlled route values is not fully implemented.

use_cases.title: Add Routing to a React App
use_cases.description: Use React Router examples to define pages, nested layouts, dynamic routes, and fallback routes.

use_cases.title: Structure a Next.js App Router Project
use_cases.description: Use file-based routing examples to organize layouts, pages, route groups, and dynamic segments.

use_cases.title: Preserve UI State in URLs
use_cases.description: Use query parameter patterns for tabs, filters, pagination, and shareable deep links.

prompt_templates.title: Create Basic Routes
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner setup for a small React app.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the web-navigation skill to add basic React Router routes for Home, About, Users, User Detail, and Not Found pages.

prompt_templates.title: Build Nested Navigation
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the web-navigation skill to design nested dashboard routes with a shared layout, sidebar, index route, analytics page, and settings page.

prompt_templates.title: Add Deep Link State
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the web-navigation skill to store product filters, sorting, pagination, and active tabs in URL query parameters.

prompt_templates.title: Review Routing Architecture
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the web-navigation skill to review my React navigation design for dynamic routes, auth guards, redirects, error states, and scroll restoration.

output_examples.input: Add navigation for a dashboard with analytics and settings.
output_examples.output: A route plan with a shared dashboard layout, index page, analytics route, settings route, and a navigation menu that marks the active link.

output_examples.input: Make product filters shareable through the URL.
output_examples.output: A query parameter strategy that maps category, sort order, and page number to URL state and updates the route when filters change.

output_examples.input: Plan a Next.js App Router structure for public and protected pages.
output_examples.output: A folder structure using route groups for public auth pages and protected dashboard pages, with shared layouts for each section.

best_practices: Validate redirect targets before navigating to user-controlled values.
Store shareable UI state in URL parameters instead of local component state.
Use framework link components for internal navigation and prefetch behavior.
anti_patterns: Do not use full page reloads for normal internal React navigation.
Do not hide important filter or tab state outside the URL when users need shareable links.
Do not copy redirect examples into production without allowlist validation.

faq.question: Does this skill install or run routing packages?
faq.answer: No. It provides patterns and examples for using routing tools in an existing React or Next.js project.

faq.question: Which routing systems does it cover?
faq.answer: It covers React Router v6 and the Next.js App Router.

faq.question: Can it help with protected routes?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes examples for protected route wrappers and route group organization.

faq.question: Does it handle query parameters?
faq.answer: Yes. It shows patterns for filters, tabs, pagination, and deep links.

faq.question: Is the redirect example production ready?
faq.answer: No. Redirect targets should be validated against trusted internal paths before production use.

faq.question: Can I use it with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill metadata lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
